Always follow these coding principles:

- Apply DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle strictly
- Write clean, maintainable, and reusable code
- Write tests. Don't use mocks. Use the helper functions and check for other tests files to see how to write tests for the project.
- Suggest abstractions when you detect code duplication
- Use meaningful variable and function names
- Add comments only for complex logic, not for obvious code
- Keep functions small and focused on a single responsibility
- Always check for existing utilities before suggesting new ones
